name: Vibrio sensor (LuxR/3OC6-HSL + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects Vibrio-type contamination via the 3-oxo-C6-homoserine-lactone quorum signal. The classic LuxR receptor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a luminescent reporter, in safe E. coli K-12.

input.detects: The LuxI-type AHL quorum signal produced by Vibrio fischeri and related Vibrio species
input.operatingRange: LuxR responds from low nM AHL

sensing.sensorMechanism: LuxR binds 3OC6-HSL and activates the Plux promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: 3OC6-HSL -> LuxR activates Plux -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a luminescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> luminescence when Vibrio AHL is present.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak luminescent reporter promoter

output.result: Luminescence indicates Vibrio-type AHL in the sample.

performance.notes: LuxR/Plux is the canonical AHL sensor; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template. Detects the signal molecule, not a live organism.

safety.biocontainment: Safe E. coli K-12 reads a secreted small molecule; no Vibrio culture required.
safety.notes: Relevant to water and seafood-safety surveillance.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak luminescent reporter promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: LuxR. TU2: Plux -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> luxCDABE.

buildSteps.title: Add sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the water/seafood rinse + AHL standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the LuxR/3OC6-HSL quorum module with E. coli CRISPRa
partsSources: Engebrecht & Silverman / lux quorum sensing (V. fischeri)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
